candidates.title: Encoder-Driven Feature Absorption: Mechanism and Safety Implications

collisions.paper: Chanin et al., 2024. A is for Absorption. arXiv:2409.14507
collisions.overlap: Documents absorption phenomenon; assumes decoder geometry drives absorption
collisions.differentiation: Our H_Mech factorial directly refutes decoder assumption: Condition B (trained encoder, random decoder) = 0.490 equals Condition D (both trained) = 0.484, while Condition C (random encoder, trained decoder) = 0.299 equals Condition A (both random) = 0.299. B>D anomaly is new finding.

collisions.paper: Tang et al., 2025. A Unified Theory of Sparse Dictionary Learning. arXiv:2512.05534
collisions.overlap: Theoretical grounding for encoder-driven local minima in SAEs
collisions.differentiation: Tang provides theory; we provide experimental factorial validation + safety implications + downstream utility test (H_Downstream)

collisions.paper: Korznikov et al., 2026. Sanity Checks for SAEs. arXiv:2602.14111
collisions.differentiation: Their comparison is across SAEs; ours is within-SAE encoder vs decoder decomposition

collisions.paper: Basu et al., 2026. Interpretability without Actionability. arXiv:2603.18353
collisions.differentiation: Basu raises the concern; we provide empirical methodology to test whether absorption is the mechanism

collisions.paper: Hu et al., 2025. Measuring SAE Feature Sensitivity. arXiv:2509.23717
collisions.differentiation: We combine sensitivity with absorption analysis; their work is standalone measurement

candidates.differentiation_notes: The 2x2 factorial decomposition (encoder vs decoder contribution) is genuinely novel methodology. No prior work has decomposed absorption this way. The B>D anomaly (trained encoder + random decoder > both trained) is a new finding. H_Downstream (from contrarian) directly tests whether absorption degrades downstream utility -- this is the highest-value new question.

candidates.title: Safety-Critical Features Are Disproportionately Absorbed

collisions.paper: Basu et al., 2026. Interpretability without Actionability. arXiv:2603.18353
collisions.overlap: Raises concern that interpretability tools may fail for safety-critical cases
collisions.differentiation: Basu argues the problem exists theoretically; we empirically test whether absorption specifically is the mechanism causing safety feature failure. No prior work performs this empirical test.
General safety feature context; no specific absorption claims

candidates.differentiation_notes: Genuinely novel: No prior work empirically tests whether safety-critical features are disproportionately absorbed in SAEs. The combination of (1) Neuronpedia-annotated safety features (validated, not arbitrary), (2) multi-child proportional absorption measurement, and (3) Mann-Whitney U test comparing safety vs non-safety is unique. CRITICAL: Must use validated Neuronpedia indices, NOT arbitrary indices (1024, 2048, etc. from invalid prior pilot).

candidates.title: Encoder Regularization to Reduce Absorption

collisions.paper: Korznikov et al., 2025. OrtSAE: Orthogonal Sparse Autoencoders. arXiv:2509.22033
collisions.overlap: Orthogonal SAE training to reduce absorption by modifying both encoder and decoder
collisions.differentiation: OrtSAE modifies both encoder and decoder; our approach is encoder-only modification, which is a cleaner intervention if H_Mech is correct (encoder drives absorption). The B>D anomaly suggests decoder acts as implicit regularizer, so encoder-only regularization is theoretically motivated.
Tang provides theory; we provide constructive intervention

candidates.differentiation_notes: Must clearly differentiate from OrtSAE by focusing on encoder-only intervention (not both). Emphasize theoretical basis from H_Mech (encoder drives absorption). Target <5% reconstruction degradation threshold. Leverage B>D anomaly as theoretical motivation.

candidates.title: Training-Free Absorption Proxy Validation

collisions.paper: Hu et al., 2025. Measuring SAE Feature Sensitivity. arXiv:2509.23717
collisions.differentiation: Sensitivity is one candidate proxy; we validate multiple candidates (ECD, RER, AED, FSS) against SynthSAEBench ground truth

collisions.overlap: Ground-truth synthetic benchmark with absorption labels
collisions.differentiation: SynthSAEBench provides validation data; we perform the systematic proxy validation study

collisions.overlap: Shows proxy metrics don't predict practical performance
collisions.differentiation: SAEBench identifies the problem; we provide systematic solution via proxy validation

candidates.differentiation_notes: First systematic validation: No prior work validates training-free proxy metrics against ground truth on SynthSAEBench. Clear falsifiable criterion: AUC > 0.75. Addresses direct field need for deep-layer absorption detection where ablation fails.

candidates.title: Multi-Resolution SAE Ensemble for Hierarchical Feature Recovery

collisions.differentiation: L0 diversity mechanism (16, 64, 256) is the claimed differentiator, but the core ensemble concept overlaps with Gadgil

collisions.overlap: Hierarchical SAE with explicit parent-child constraints

candidates.differentiation_notes: Proposal correctly states 'WEAK BACKUP due to overlap with Gadgil et al. and high cost.' Only proceed if H_Downstream shows absorption DOES matter AND encoder regularization fails AND clear differentiation from Gadgil is demonstrated.

key_collisions.chanin_2024: partial_overlap - absorption phenomenon documented, assumes decoder drives (we refute with factorial decomposition)
key_collisions.tang_2512.05534: related_work - theoretical foundation for encoder geometry, does not provide experimental validation or safety analysis
key_collisions.korznikov_2025_ortsae: partial_overlap - modifies both encoder+decoder, we target encoder only
key_collisions.korznikov_2026: related_work - sanity checks methodology, cross-SAE comparison vs our within-SAE decomposition
key_collisions.basu_2603.18353: related_work - raises safety concern theoretically, we test absorption as mechanism empirically
key_collisions.gadgil_2025: partial_overlap - general ensemble concept, L0 diversity is differentiator but core idea overlaps
key_collisions.hu_2025: related_work - sensitivity measurement, we validate it as proxy against ground truth
key_collisions.synthsaebench_2026: related_work - provides ground truth data, we perform validation study on it
key_collisions.saebench_2025: related_work - identifies proxy metrics fail, we address by systematic validation

added: H_Downstream (from contrarian perspective added to cand_p1)

anti_patterns_avoided: Did not rubber-stamp novelty without searching - each claim checked against prior work
Did not dismiss ideas due to vaguely related work - precise overlap classification
Did not conflate related_work with already_done - same area ≠ collision
Did not skip candidates - all 5 assessed
critical_warnings: cand_safe pilot used INVALID arbitrary indices (1024, 2048, 3072, etc.) - must use Neuronpedia-validated features ONLY
H_Pareto (sensitivity-absorption frontier) SUSPENDED until formula bug is fixed - sensitivity values exceed [0,1] bounds
cand_ens is correctly deferred due to overlap with Gadgil - do not revive unless clear differentiation
